page: Amazon Web Services trn1.2xlarge is an x86_64 virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided by a 474 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 3 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the trn1.2xlarge demonstrates mid-tier performance across most standard compute and memory tasks. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1892.38, and the multi-core score is 7730.77, both falling into the average category. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 416.53 GB/sec and a large block uncached speed of 55.71 GB/sec. For database operations, the instance achieves 7.97 million Redis SET operations per second and a PassMark database score of 4012.91.

Performance drops significantly during complex language model tasks on CPU. While prompt processing for a small 135M LLM model reaches 566.23 tokens per second, a medium 7B model drops to 16.34 tokens per second, which is categorized as poor. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is also relatively weak at 31.76 GB/sec. Given its hardware profile, the instance offers moderate resource density and cost efficiency for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and basic database operations, but is less efficient for heavy multi-threaded decompression or larger local LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services trn1.2xlarge is an x86_64 virtual server built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a 3.5 GHz clock speed. It is configured with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a 474 GB local NVMe SSD, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1634.0 and multi-core score of 6055.0. However, it exhibits weak performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and poor efficiency in medium-scale LLM inference. This instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and moderate database operations where a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio is required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services trn1.2xlarge is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 474 GB NVMe SSD, delivering balanced performance for general-purpose workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, balanced memory density, and local NVMe SSD storage for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 3.5 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
474 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average performance in standard compute, memory bandwidth, and database benchmarks
Reduced efficiency in multi-threaded decompression and medium-scale LLM inference
Suitable for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and database operations
